version**
1.2 (and dev)

**Bug description**
The calculation of the mean energy and following also the energy bias uses `pdf = self.data[idx]` which is not a PDF for the EDispKernel, but rather an array of raw probabilities for reconstructing events in the corresponding ereco bins. I think this method only gives the correct result in case of a spectrum which results in the same number of events in each bin. 
In the general case I think the PDF needs to take into account the different bin sizes, i.e., `pdf = self.data[idx]/np.diff(self.axes["energy"].edges)`, resulting in a true PDF giving dP/dE.

I found that the energy bias computed by the EDispKernel generally predicts a larger bias compared to the calculation based on the simulated events directly. This is explained by the wrong PDF giving too much weight to the higher energy bin, as the log bins increase in size.

version**
Gammapy: v1.1
Astropy: v5.3.1

**Bug description**
In the AIT projection the `coord_to_pix()` method does not return the edge pixels when evaluated on the edge coordinates. This effect is especially large when using a all-sky geometry. I noticed this when the interpolation to a CAR geometry cutted the edge of the map.

**Expected behavior**
```
from gammapy.maps import WcsGeom
geom=WcsGeom.create(proj="CAR", binsz=(360/1001,180/201))
print(geom)
print(geom.coord_to_pix(dict(lon=[-180,0,180]*u.deg, lat=[0]*u.deg)))
print(geom.coord_to_pix(dict(lon=[0]*u.deg, lat=[-90,0,90]*u.deg)))
```
returns
```
WcsGeom

	axes       : ['lon', 'lat']
	shape      : (1001, 201)
	ndim       : 2
	frame      : icrs
	projection : CAR
	center     : 0.0 deg, 0.0 deg
	width      : 360.0 deg x 180.0 deg
	wcs ref    : 0.0 deg, 0.0 deg

(array([ 1.0005e+03,  5.0000e+02, -5.0000e-01]), array([100., 100., 100.]))
(array([500., 500., 500.]), array([ -0.5, 100. , 200.5]))
```
So the edge coordinates are mapped to the edge pixels.
**To Reproduce**
```
from gammapy.maps import WcsGeom
geom=WcsGeom.create(proj="AIT", binsz=(360/1001,180/201))
print(geom)
print(geom.coord_to_pix(dict(lon=[-180,0,180]*u.deg, lat=[0]*u.deg)))
print(geom.coord_to_pix(dict(lon=[0]*u.deg, lat=[-90,0,90]*u.deg)))
```
returns
```
WcsGeom

	axes       : ['lon', 'lat']
	shape      : (1001, 201)
	ndim       : 2
	frame      : icrs
	projection : AIT
	center     : 0.0 deg, 0.0 deg
	width      : 360.0 deg x 180.0 deg
	wcs ref    : 0.0 deg, 0.0 deg

(array([950.60831624, 500.        ,  49.39168376]), array([100., 100., 100.]))
(array([500., 500., 500.]), array([  9.51821023, 100.        , 190.48178977]))
```

**Other information**
While I expect this behavior for latitudes other than 0, I don't understand why the edge coordinates at lat=0 should not map the edge pixels. I think this might be a bug also because the interpolated map did not contain the edge anymore as those pixels were never interpolated.

axis","user":{"login":"tunbehaun273","id":60741323,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYwNzQxMzIz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60741323?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273","html_url":"https://github.com/tunbehaun273","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tunbehaun273/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":39740769,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzOTc0MDc2OQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/labels/bug","name":"bug","color":"fc2929","default":true,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-07-29T19:07:50Z","updated_at":"2023-07-29T19:07:50Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The current version of [gammapy/irf/edisp/map.py/get_overlap_fraction](https://github.com/gammapy/gammapy/blob/v1.2.dev/gammapy/irf/edisp/map.py#L11) fails to give proper values when the unit of the true and reco axis are not the same as the unit (i.e. MeV/TeV) is returned. This affects for example the `from_diagonal_response` of the EDispKernel.

As solution I would suggest to implement the following:
```
def get_overlap_fraction(energy_axis, energy_axis_true):
    a_min = energy_axis.edges[:-1]
    a_max = energy_axis.edges[1:]

    b_min = energy_axis_true.edges[:-1][:, np.newaxis]
    b_max = energy_axis_true.edges[1:][:, np.newaxis]

    xmin = np.fmin(a_max, b_max)
    xmax = np.fmax(a_min, b_min)
    fractions = np.clip(xmin - xmax, 0, np.inf) / (b_max - b_min)
    return fractions.to_value('')
```

will probably fix the issue for most cases but I think the lines `if changed:
            self._cached_parameter_values = values` might also be problematic in some cases.
For the caching of the UnbinnedEvaluator (https://github.com/gammapy/gammapy-unbinned-analysis/blob/main/EventDataset/UnbinnedEvaluator.py) I went for a different approach: 

1. I check if a parameter changed and if this is only the norm parameter **but** I do not reset the cached parameter values
2. Dependent on the case I return the cache or simply rescale the cache or fully re-compute the cache.
3. Only after the cache is rescaled or re-computed I set the cached parameter values to the ones for which the cache is computed.
Like this I also don't need the "previous" parameters for renormalization. To me this approach feels more natural and robust but maybe I am also missing some reasons for the existing method.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/issues/comments/1437057676/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

version**
version 0.19


**Bug description**
Interpolating a Map using interp_by_coord() gives the same result independent of the method. The method keyword is actually passed to the ScaledRegularGridInterpolator but it doesn't seem to make any difference. The interpolation is linear. Other keywords like points_scale and values_scale are not passed but probably should be i.e. an energy axis should be interpolated in log scale.

**Expected behavior**
Actually performing a nearest or linear interpolation for the respective method. Passing the correct scales to ScaledRegularGridInterpolator.

**To Reproduce**
```
import numpy as np
from gammapy.maps import Map, MapAxis

axis = MapAxis.from_energy_bounds("1 TeV", "10 TeV", nbin=3)

m = Map.create(npix=(3, 3))
m.data += np.arange(9).reshape((3, 3))

print(m.interp_by_coord({"lon": 0.07, "lat": 0.03}, method="linear"))
print(m.interp_by_coord({"lon": 0.07, "lat": 0.03}, method="nearest"))
```
